issue: GDPR Partial: Data portability only covers course/package exports, not subject-level personal data export
root_cause: Export implementation is domain-specific (CourseExportController/export services) and there is no unified data-subject aggregation service spanning user, billing, auth, and usage repositories.
solution: Add a GDPR portability module with DataPortabilityController (GET /v1/privacy/me/export), DataPortabilityService, and repository adapters. Use async export orchestration (job table + signed download URL) and DTO-based schema (PersonalDataExportDto) that includes profile, billing contacts, auth events, and API usage.
code_changes: Controller: new DataPortabilityController secured with @PreAuthorize("isAuthenticated()"); Service: DataPortabilityService.buildExport(userId) pulls from UserRepository, BillingContactRepository, ApiUsageRepository, etc.; Config: register endpoint under security matcher; Pseudo: PersonalDataExportDto dto = assembler.from(user, contacts, usage, payments); then serialize JSON/CSV package.
data_changes: DB: add privacy_export_jobs (id, user_id, status, expires_at, checksum); Redis: cache export job progress with TTL (e.g., 24h) to avoid polling DB hot paths; Riak: optional object bucket for large export artifact chunks with lifecycle metadata.
security_improvements: Require fresh JWT + optional step-up check before export generation, sign download links, enforce one-user access boundary, and audit each export request/result.
compliance_mapping: GDPR Art. 20 (Data Portability), SOC2 CC6/CC7 (access and processing control), ISO 27001 A.5/A.8 (information access and handling)

issue: GDPR Partial: Data minimization is inconsistent across DTOs
root_cause: DTOs validate format but not necessity; controllers accept broad payloads without purpose-scoped field policies, and services do not enforce context-aware field whitelists.
solution: Introduce purpose-scoped request models and validation groups (@Validated(CreateProfile.class) etc.). Add a DataMinimizationPolicyService enforcing allowed fields per endpoint purpose and role.
code_changes: Controller: replace generic DTOs with purpose-specific DTOs (BillingContactUpdateDto, TenantOnboardingDto, etc.); Service: add validateAllowedFields(purpose, payload) before persistence; Config: add custom ConstraintValidator for prohibited-field combinations.
data_changes: DB: no mandatory schema change, but optionally split rarely used sensitive attributes into separate table with strict access path; Redis: cache minimization policy map keyed by endpoint+role with short TTL.
security_improvements: Reduce attack surface from over-posting/mass assignment, enforce strict JSON property binding (FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=true for sensitive endpoints), and reject unnecessary PII ingestion.
compliance_mapping: GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) Data Minimization, SOC2 CC3/CC6, ISO 27001 A.8 (information classification/handling)

issue: GDPR Partial: Purpose limitation is implicit, not explicit/enforced
root_cause: Processing purpose is embedded in business logic and not represented as a first-class policy attribute in API/service layers.
solution: Add purpose-tag propagation from API to service (ProcessingPurpose enum). Enforce via AOP interceptor (@RequiresPurpose) and policy matrix service.
code_changes: Controller: annotate endpoints with @RequiresPurpose(Purpose.BILLING_CONTACT_UPDATE); Service: accept ProcessingContext with purpose and actor; Aspect: block unauthorized purpose/data combinations before method execution.
data_changes: DB: add processing_purpose and legal_basis columns to audit records; Redis: cache purpose policy matrix and invalidate on policy change; Riak: include purpose metadata in object headers for immutable audit context.
security_improvements: Centralized purpose checks prevent function creep, improve traceability, and harden against unauthorized secondary use of collected data.
compliance_mapping: GDPR Art. 5(1)(b) Purpose Limitation, SOC2 CC2/CC7, ISO 27001 A.5/A.8

issue: GDPR Missing: Consent tracking ledger/versioning not implemented
root_cause: No consent domain model, no evented consent capture endpoint, and no versioned policy references tied to user actions.
solution: Implement ConsentController + ConsentService with versioned consent records (policy_version, purpose, status, timestamp, source_ip, user_agent). Use append-only consent events and immutable hash chain for integrity.
code_changes: Controller: POST /v1/privacy/consents and GET /v1/privacy/consents; Service: recordConsent(command) and withdrawConsent(...); DAO: ConsentRepository; add @PreAuthorize("isAuthenticated()") and service-level ownership checks.
data_changes: DB: new user_consents and consent_events tables with unique index (user_id,purpose,policy_version); Redis: cache current consent state per user/purpose TTL 15m for fast policy checks; Riak: store consent event snapshots for long-term immutable retention if required.
security_improvements: Sign consent payload digest, enforce write-once event records, and include consent status check in business flows before processing optional personal data.
compliance_mapping: GDPR Art. 6/7 (lawful basis and consent), SOC2 CC6/CC8, ISO 27001 A.5/A.8/A.12

issue: GDPR Missing: Comprehensive subject-access endpoint not implemented
root_cause: No DSAR orchestration layer; data access remains fragmented by bounded contexts and endpoint ownership.
solution: Add DSAR APIs (/v1/privacy/me/access-report) producing machine-readable + human-readable reports. Orchestrate with service fan-out and standardized mappers.
code_changes: Controller: DataSubjectAccessController; Service: DsarAggregationService; DAO: repository adapters returning scoped projections; add request throttling and asynchronous job processing.
data_changes: DB: dsar_requests table for status, SLA clock, and fulfillment logs; Redis: DSAR workflow state TTL and idempotency keys; Riak: archive generated reports with retention policy metadata.
security_improvements: Strong requester verification, request anti-replay, per-user scoping, signed report artifacts, and complete DSAR audit trail.
compliance_mapping: GDPR Art. 15 (Right of Access), SOC2 CC6/CC7, ISO 27001 A.5/A.8

issue: GDPR Missing: Centralized retention/deletion policy engine for PII
root_cause: Retention is implemented in isolated services (file/session cleanup) without global policy registry and entity-level enforcement.
solution: Implement retention policy engine with declarative rules (RetentionPolicy), scheduled executor, and deletion/anonymization handlers per entity type.
code_changes: Config: RetentionPolicyConfig loads policy from DB/YAML; Service: RetentionEngineService.run(); DAO: retention queries per repository; add @Scheduled orchestrator + dead-letter handling.
data_changes: DB: retention_policies and retention_execution_log; Redis: lock keys (SETNX) for singleton scheduler and per-batch checkpoints TTL; Riak: use bucket-level lifecycle tags and delete markers.
security_improvements: Deterministic expiry handling, reduced stale-PII risk, tamper-evident execution logs, and controlled purge workflows with dual-control for sensitive domains.
compliance_mapping: GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) Storage Limitation & Art. 17, SOC2 CC8, ISO 27001 A.8/A.12

issue: SOC2 Partial: Access control split between permitAll filter chain and custom interceptor/manual checks
root_cause: Spring Security is not the single enforcement point; authorization logic is duplicated in interceptors/controllers, creating policy drift.
solution: Move to SecurityFilterChain-first enforcement: deny-by-default, JWT auth filter (OncePerRequestFilter), and standardized exception handling (AuthenticationEntryPoint, AccessDeniedHandler).
code_changes: Config: replace .anyRequest().permitAll() with explicit permit list + .anyRequest().authenticated(); Security: add JwtAuthenticationFilter populating SecurityContext; Controller/Service: remove manual bearer parsing and rely on @AuthenticationPrincipal.
data_changes: Redis: maintain token jti blacklist/session-state cache TTL to support revocation and logout; DB optional: revoked_tokens for durable revocation.
security_improvements: Single policy plane, fewer bypass paths, consistent 401/403 behavior, and stricter endpoint hardening.
compliance_mapping: SOC2 CC6 (logical access), ISO 27001 A.5/A.8, GDPR Art. 32

issue: SOC2 Partial: RBAC exists but is uneven and not centralized
root_cause: Role checks are mixed (@PreAuthorize in few places, manual role parsing elsewhere) and no unified authorization policy service exists.
solution: Enable method security globally and standardize role/attribute checks with SpEL policies + custom PermissionEvaluator.
code_changes: Config: @EnableMethodSecurity; Service/Controller: annotate privileged operations (@PreAuthorize("hasRole('SUPER_ADMIN') or @perm.canAccessWorkspace(authentication,#workspaceId)")); add PermissionEvaluator and workspace-scope voter.
data_changes: DB: normalize role-permission matrix tables (roles, permissions, role_permissions); Redis: cache permission graph by role with cache invalidation on admin changes.
security_improvements: Consistent least-privilege authorization, context-aware access checks, and reduction of ad-hoc role parsing bugs.
compliance_mapping: SOC2 CC6.1/CC6.2, ISO 27001 A.5 (access control), GDPR Art. 32

issue: SOC2 Partial: Audit logging coverage is limited to selected actions
root_cause: Audit event publication is manual and sparse; no cross-cutting mechanism captures high-risk CRUD/auth/privilege actions consistently.
solution: Implement audit as cross-cutting concern using AOP (@Auditable) plus security event listeners and persistence-backed audit store.
code_changes: Create @Auditable(action="..."); Aspect captures actor, target, before/after hash, request ID; Service layer annotates critical methods; add async AuditWriter with SLF4J structured JSON output.
data_changes: DB: audit_log table partitioned by date; Redis: short-term stream/buffer for burst writes; Riak: immutable archive bucket for long-retention audit records.
security_improvements: Tamper-resistant logs (hash chain/signature), full traceability for sensitive operations, and easier incident reconstruction.
compliance_mapping: SOC2 CC7/CC8, ISO 27001 A.12 (logging/monitoring), GDPR Art. 30/32

issue: SOC2 Missing: Redis caching controls absent because Redis integration is absent
root_cause: No cache architecture was implemented for security-sensitive state (revocation, rate limiting, ephemeral risk controls).
solution: Add Redis with Spring Data Redis for token revocation, rate limiting, and ephemeral policy caches. Define cache namespaces and security TTL standards.
code_changes: Config: RedisConfig (LettuceConnectionFactory, RedisTemplate, cache manager); Security: TokenRevocationService using jti keys; Filter: rate-limit filter reading Redis counters.
data_changes: Redis keys: auth:revoked:{jti} TTL=token-exp, rl:login:{ip} sliding window TTL, policy:rbac:{role} TTL=15m; DB remains source of truth; Riak unaffected for this control.
security_improvements: Immediate revocation capability, brute-force mitigation, reduced stale authorization decisions.
compliance_mapping: SOC2 CC6/CC7, ISO 27001 A.8/A.12, GDPR Art. 32

issue: SOC2 Missing: Riak storage controls absent because Riak integration is absent
root_cause: No object-storage strategy exists for immutable security/compliance artifacts (audit snapshots, DSAR archives, consent snapshots).
solution: Introduce Riak adapter layer for append-only compliance artifacts with bucket types enforcing replication and conflict policies.
code_changes: Create RiakClientConfig and RiakComplianceStore service; write through service APIs only (storeAuditSnapshot, storeExportArtifact); maintain repository abstraction to avoid controller-level storage calls.
data_changes: Riak buckets: audit-archive, privacy-export, consent-snapshot; store encrypted payload + metadata (owner, purpose, retention_until); Redis caches object pointers for fast retrieval; DB stores index/reference records.
security_improvements: Durable immutable archival path, separation of operational DB from compliance evidence, and safer long-term artifact retention.
compliance_mapping: SOC2 CC8/CC9, ISO 27001 A.8/A.12, GDPR Art. 30/32

issue: SOC2 Missing: Centralized monitoring/tracing instrumentation not implemented
root_cause: Monitoring is mostly health endpoint + app logs; no standardized metrics, tracing, or alert-grade security telemetry emission.
solution: Adopt Micrometer + Spring Boot Actuator metrics and structured security telemetry pipeline. Add security KPIs and incident-oriented events.
code_changes: Config: expose metrics endpoint internally; Service/Filter: publish counters (auth_failures, token_revocations, access_denied); add HandlerInterceptor for latency/status tagging and correlation-id propagation.
data_changes: Redis: optional rolling counters for near-real-time anomaly checks; Riak: store periodic immutable monitoring snapshots for audit evidence; DB: optional security_incidents table.
security_improvements: Faster detection of abuse/misconfiguration, measurable control effectiveness, and better forensic readiness.
compliance_mapping: SOC2 CC7 (monitoring), ISO 27001 A.12/A.16, GDPR Art. 32

issue: ISO27001 Partial: Encryption practice is mixed (BCrypt good, AES utility weak)
root_cause: Symmetric crypto utility uses hardcoded key and ECB mode, likely introduced as convenience without centralized crypto policy.
solution: Replace with envelope encryption using AES-GCM (Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding")) and key material from KMS/Vault. Standardize via CryptoService bean.
code_changes: Service: CryptoService.encrypt/decrypt with random IV per record and auth tag; Config: inject active key id from env/secret manager; replace direct EncryptionUtil calls in auth/user services.
data_changes: DB/Riak: store {keyId, iv, ciphertext, tag} per encrypted field/object; Redis: never store plaintext secrets; cache only encrypted blobs or opaque references with TTL.
security_improvements: Confidentiality + integrity protection, removal of static key exposure, and cryptographic agility via key ids.
compliance_mapping: ISO 27001 A.8/A.10, SOC2 CC6.6, GDPR Art. 32

issue: ISO27001 Partial: Secure configuration has unsafe defaults (useSSL=false, ddl-auto=update)
root_cause: Environment hardening boundaries are not enforced by profile-specific config governance and startup validation.
solution: Implement secure-by-default profile policy with startup guard rails (@ConfigurationProperties + validator) that fail fast on unsafe production settings.
code_changes: Config: SecurityBaselineProperties + @PostConstruct checks (if(prod && !dbSsl) throw); Security config: tighten actuator and CORS defaults; remove dangerous fallbacks from prod properties.
data_changes: DB: enforce TLS connection parameters and cert validation; Redis/Riak clients configured with TLS and auth in production profiles.
security_improvements: Prevents insecure runtime drift, ensures encrypted transport to data stores, and blocks accidental weak deployments.
compliance_mapping: ISO 27001 A.5/A.8/A.12, SOC2 CC5/CC6, GDPR Art. 32

issue: ISO27001 Partial: Risk controls are selective, not codified uniformly
root_cause: Security controls are spread across ad-hoc checks and comments; no policy-as-code baseline across modules.
solution: Create security control framework: baseline annotations, centralized validators, and CI policy checks for critical controls.
code_changes: Add @SensitiveOperation + interceptor enforcing MFA freshness/role checks where required; add centralized SecurityPolicyService; add build-time checks (e.g., ArchUnit) enforcing controller/service security rules.
data_changes: Redis: store control state (e.g., risk flags/session trust level) with TTL; Riak: archive periodic policy compliance snapshots.
security_improvements: Consistent risk treatment, fewer control gaps in new modules, and auditable enforcement semantics.
compliance_mapping: ISO 27001 A.5/A.6/A.12, SOC2 CC3/CC6/CC8

issue: ISO27001 Missing: Secrets management pattern not compliant (secrets in properties)
root_cause: Application reads static secrets from repo-managed property files instead of runtime secret sources.
solution: Externalize all secrets to Vault/KMS/secret manager and inject via environment or Spring Cloud Vault; keep properties as placeholders only.
code_changes: Config: replace literal secrets with ${...} references; add SecretProvider abstraction for JWT/DB/API keys; startup check rejects default/test secret values in non-dev profiles.
data_changes: DB: rotate credentials and move to short-lived auth where possible; Redis/Riak credentials sourced from secret manager and rotated regularly.
security_improvements: Eliminates credential leakage from SCM, supports rotation, and reduces blast radius of compromised artifacts.
compliance_mapping: ISO 27001 A.5/A.8, SOC2 CC6.1/CC6.6, GDPR Art. 32

issue: ISO27001 Missing: No key rotation/revocation lifecycle for JWT/API secrets
root_cause: Single static key model is used; JWT validation does not support multi-key (kid) or revocation lifecycle.
solution: Implement key management lifecycle: JWKS-style key ring, active/previous keys, scheduled rotation, and emergency revocation.
code_changes: Security: add JwtKeyProvider supporting kid; token minting adds header kid; validation resolves key by kid; add admin/internal endpoint to mark keys revoked and trigger cache invalidation.
data_changes: Redis: keys:active, keys:revoked:{kid} with TTL; DB: security_keys table (kid, status, created_at, revoked_at); Riak: immutable key-rotation audit records.
security_improvements: Supports seamless rotation, fast revocation after compromise, and reduced long-term key exposure risk.
compliance_mapping: ISO 27001 A.10/A.12, SOC2 CC6/CC7, GDPR Art. 32

issue: ISO27001 Missing: No systematic field-level encryption for sensitive persisted data
root_cause: PII is stored in plaintext in operational entities; encryption is not enforced by persistence layer policy.
solution: Apply transparent field encryption with JPA AttributeConverter or service-level crypto wrappers for marked fields (@EncryptedField).
code_changes: Entity/DAO: annotate sensitive fields (email/phone/address/tokens) and bind converters; Service: decrypt only at boundary where needed; add masking in DTO mappers for non-privileged responses.
data_changes: DB: migrate existing plaintext to encrypted columns via controlled migration job; Redis: store only masked/minimal PII; Riak: encrypt object payload before write and maintain keyId metadata.
security_improvements: Data-at-rest hardening for PII, reduced impact from DB dumps, and stronger compartmentalization of sensitive values.
compliance_mapping: ISO 27001 A.8/A.10, SOC2 CC6.6, GDPR Art. 32

issue: Cross-cutting Risk: Sensitive data appears in logs and exception messages
root_cause: Controllers log request-associated values and concatenate raw exception messages into API responses; no centralized redaction policy.
solution: Adopt structured logging with redaction filters and standardized error translation (@ControllerAdvice) returning sanitized error codes.
code_changes: Logging: add MaskingPatternLayout/MDC redaction strategy; Web: global exception handler maps internal exceptions to safe payloads; remove direct logging of URLs/session IDs/PII fields in controllers.
data_changes: Redis: optional temporary correlation-id map for incident tracing without storing raw payload; Riak: long-term audit logs store masked fields only.
security_improvements: Prevents PII/token leakage via logs/responses, improves incident handling without exposing sensitive context.
compliance_mapping: GDPR Art. 5(1)(f)/32, SOC2 CC6/CC7, ISO 27001 A.12

issue: GDPR Missing: Personal data inventory and access registry is not centralized
root_cause: Personal data usage is spread across DTOs/entities/services without a single inventory mapping data category, storage location, access scope, and processor context.
solution: Implement a Data Inventory Registry module that maps personal/sensitive data fields to source endpoints, storage systems, retention period, and authorized roles; keep registry queryable for audits.
code_changes: Add DataInventoryController (internal/compliance), DataInventoryService, and metadata scanners for DTO/entity annotations; add role-restricted endpoints to view inventory and access map.
data_changes: DB: data_inventory_catalog table with fields for data_type, sensitivity, purpose, storage_system, retention_rule, access_roles, processor_reference; Redis optional cache for lookup.
security_improvements: Improves accountability, least-privilege reviews, and rapid impact analysis during incidents or data-subject requests.
compliance_mapping: GDPR accountability/Art. 30 readiness, SOC2 CC3/CC6, ISO 27001 A.5/A.8

issue: GDPR Missing: Minors (under 16) consent and age-gating controls are not implemented
root_cause: Registration/data collection flows do not model age verification or parental authorization conditions.
solution: Add age-threshold validation and parental consent workflow for minors, including status transitions and verification evidence retention.
code_changes: Update relevant registration/profile DTOs and controllers with age fields/flags; add MinorConsentService and guard checks before processing optional personal data.
data_changes: DB: user_age_verification and parental_consent_records tables; Redis for pending verification/session TTL.
security_improvements: Reduces unlawful processing risk for minors and enforces stricter controls over sensitive user segments.
compliance_mapping: GDPR Art. 8 (children consent), SOC2 CC6, ISO 27001 A.8

issue: GDPR Missing: Cookie and form consent lifecycle controls are not comprehensively implemented
root_cause: Consent capture is not standardized for cookie categories, form privacy notices, correspondence opt-in, and consent withdrawal history.
solution: Implement Consent Preference Center backend APIs for cookie categories, form consent proof, correspondence preferences, and withdrawal/change history.
code_changes: Add ConsentPreferenceController and FormConsentService; enforce consent checks in data-collection endpoints and messaging workflows.
data_changes: DB: consent_preferences, form_consent_events, correspondence_preferences; Redis for active consent cache with durable DB source.
security_improvements: Creates auditable proof of consent state and persistent opt-out enforcement.
compliance_mapping: GDPR consent transparency/opt-in expectations, SOC2 CC7/CC8, ISO 27001 A.12

issue: GDPR Missing: Third-party processor and international transfer governance is not modeled
root_cause: Backend compliance artifacts do not maintain processor catalog, transfer basis, safeguards, or review status.
solution: Add Processor Governance module to register third-party processors, transfer regions, legal basis, DPA status, and transfer safeguards.
code_changes: Add ProcessorController and TransferGovernanceService with policy validation hooks for integrations that handle personal data.
data_changes: DB: processors, processor_assessments, transfer_mechanisms, transfer_risk_reviews tables; optional Riak archival of signed assessment documents.
security_improvements: Strengthens vendor oversight and cross-border transfer accountability.
compliance_mapping: GDPR Chapter V transfer governance, SOC2 CC3/CC9, ISO 27001 supplier relationship controls

issue: GDPR Missing: Breach response and 72-hour notification workflow is not implemented
root_cause: No structured incident workflow exists for breach classification, authority notification timelines, affected-user communication, and corrective action tracking.
solution: Implement Breach Response Orchestration module with incident states, timer-based SLA checks, notification templates, and evidence bundle generation.
code_changes: Add BreachIncidentController and BreachResponseService; integrate security event triggers and escalation hooks.
data_changes: DB: breach_incidents, breach_notifications, breach_actions, breach_impacted_subjects tables; Redis timer queues for SLA reminders.
security_improvements: Improves regulatory response readiness and repeatable incident handling.
compliance_mapping: GDPR Art. 33/34, SOC2 CC7/CC8, ISO 27001 incident management controls
